definition: Stock-based compensation overstates AI-sector profitability by 15-40%. Aggregate Mag-7 SBC is ~$75-80B annually, creating a GAAP vs. non-GAAP P/E gap of 10-15 turns for pure-play AI companies and 1.5-2.0 P/E turns (~$14-16/share) for the S&P 500. A reflexive loop mechanism exists where rising prices lower perceived SBC cost, improving reported margins, driving further price increases.
evidence: GAAP vs. non-GAAP P/E gap of 10-15 turns for pure-play AI companies
$27B SBC attributable to S&P 500 × 1.5 P/E turns / index divisor ≈ $14-16/share
Reflexive loop survived pair_1 debate at 9/10
GAAP P/E adjusted for SBC dilution: 35-50x vs non-GAAP 25-35x
Annual share dilution of 2-5% creates effective growth hurdle
Pair_1 debate confirmed this as essentially an accounting fact, not a forecast
open_questions: At what stock price level does the SBC reflexive loop reverse (higher prices needed to retain talent while dilution pressure mounts)?
How does SBC vega (sensitivity of reported margins to stock price) interact with index rebalancing?
At what point does SBC dilution become self-correcting via declining share prices?
How does the SBC reflexivity loop ($14-16/share S&P 500 impact) change the correction dynamics?
